subject: Q1 Order — Golden Coast
body:
Hi Kwame,

Happy new year. We'd like to place our Q1 order — 30 tons standard grade. Can you confirm pricing and send a delivery schedule?

Thanks,
David

subject: RE: Q1 Order — Golden Coast
body:
David,

Happy new year. Q1 price is $2,780/ton. I'll copy Efua Mensah, our logistics coordinator, who will confirm the schedule and handle shipment documentation.

Shall I raise the pro-forma invoice?

Kwame

subject: Delivery schedule — Q1 Strauss order
body:
Hi David,

This is Efua from logistics. For your Q1 order of 30 MT, our standard CIF lead time from Tema port to Ashdod is 12 business days from vessel loading date. We are targeting a loading date of January 26, which gives an estimated arrival at Ashdod of around February 11-13.

Please confirm and Kwame will raise the pro-forma.

Efua

subject: RE: Q1 Order — confirmed
body:
Confirmed — 30 tons at $2,780/ton. January 26 loading, February 11-13 ETA works. Please raise the PFI.

David

subject: Pro-Forma Invoice — GCC-2026-0047
body:
David,

Please find attached PFI GCC-2026-0047 for 30 MT standard grade cocoa at USD 2,780/MT. Efua will coordinate the Tema port logistics from here.

Kwame

subject: RE: Pro-Forma Invoice — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Confirmed. Proceed with shipment per the PFI.

David

subject: Shipment update — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Hi Efua,

We are past the February 13 estimated arrival and have not received the shipment or a tracking update. Can you confirm current status?

David

subject: RE: Shipment update — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Hi David,

Apologies for the delay in responding. The vessel experienced a mechanical issue at sea and was diverted to Las Palmas for inspection and repair. We are now estimating 22 business days from the original loading date of January 26 — so revised ETA at Ashdod is approximately March 5-7.

The shipping line is MedLink and they are managing the situation. We will send the updated bill of lading once the vessel departs Las Palmas.

Efua

subject: Shipment arrived — quality inspection pending
body:
Efua, Kwame,

Shipment arrived this morning. Our QC team is conducting the incoming inspection now — will revert once complete. Note we are already 3+ weeks past the original 12-business-day delivery commitment, which under our contract triggers the delay penalty clause. We will address this separately.

David

subject: Quality inspection result — ISSUE
body:
Kwame,

Our QC has completed inspection on GCC-2026-0047 and we have a problem. Moisture content came in at 8.2% on average across the sampled bags (13 bags tested), with two bags as high as 9.1%. Our spec and your contract require less than 7.5% moisture. This is out of specification.

We need to discuss how you want to handle this — we are not in a position to accept this shipment at contract price. Please respond urgently.

David

subject: RE: Quality inspection result — our position
body:
David,

Thank you for the update. I have reviewed this with our team.

I must respectfully push back on your findings. Our pre-shipment inspection at Tema on January 24 showed moisture at 6.8% — well within spec. A reading of 8.2% at destination is not consistent with what we shipped. Our position is that the moisture increase occurred during the Las Palmas diversion — extended transit in a non-standard hold environment over that period.

We shipped compliant product. We do not consider this a Golden Coast quality failure. We would request an independent third-party moisture test before any discussion of price adjustment.

Kwame

subject: RE: Quality dispute — our position
body:
Kwame,

We understand your argument on transit conditions, but our contract is clear: risk passes at destination, not at origin. The product arrived out of spec. We do not have a copy of your January 24 pre-shipment moisture report — please send it.

We are open to an independent test but we need to agree on the lab and cost allocation first. We are using this batch under protest while we resolve the dispute. We will not pay full contract price for non-conforming goods.

David

subject: RE: Quality dispute — pre-shipment certificate
body:
David,

Please see the attached pre-shipment moisture certificate from SGS Ghana (cert ref: SGS-GH-2026-00312, January 24). Result: 6.8% moisture, within spec.

We propose SGS Netherlands for the independent test, costs split equally. If the result confirms our position, we expect full payment at contract price plus the cost of the independent test.

Kwame

subject: RE: Quality dispute — update
body:
Kwame,

We have reviewed the SGS Ghana certificate. We are not disputing the product may have been within spec at loading. But our receipt test governs under the contract — risk passes at destination. We are reviewing the contract language with our legal team.

On SGS Netherlands — we can agree to that lab but the cost split only covers the test cost, not broader liability conclusions. We will revert early next week.

David

subject: RE: Quality dispute — still waiting
body:
David,

It has now been two weeks since your last message and we have not heard your final position on the independent test. We are holding the invoice pending resolution. Please advise — we cannot leave this open indefinitely.

Kwame

subject: RE: Quality dispute
body:
Kwame,

Apologies for the delay — this went to our legal team for review and has taken longer than expected. We are still finalising our position on the risk-transfer clause. I expect to have something for you by end of April.

David
